data.title: Synposys slip on guidance creates buying opportunity: analysts
data.description:
Synopsys (NASDAQ:SNPS) slipped during Thursday trading following weaker-than-expected guidance for fiscal 2025 in its latest financial results, but analysts cite it as temporary and view the slide as a buying opportunity.

"At issue is management's initial FY25 revenue guide which assumes only 10.6% growth at the midpoint (vs. the prior cons view of 12.7% growth and vs. 15% growth in FY24)," said Loop Capital analyst Gary Mobley, in an investor note.

"Also, at issue is the weighting to FY25 revenue, with 55% expected to come in 2H," he added. "These issues are partially explained by the fact FY25 has eight fewer days than FY24, with 1H FY25 having 10 fewer days than 1H FY24, in addition to 2H FY25 having more upfront revenue (e.g., more IP and hardware verification deliverables). We see these as transitory issues."

Loop reiterated its Buy rating and a $675 price target following the fourth quarter results and outlook.

Synopsys also de-risked its exposure to China early in fiscal 2024, indicating sales tied to China will dilute overall growth, Mobley added. Synposys remains on track to close its acquisition of Ansys (NASDAQ:ANSS) during the first half of calendar year 2025. Ansys is expected to increase its revenue by 13.9% during fiscal year 2024 versus 9.5% growth in the year prior.

Needham also pointed out that guidance missed most estimates due to the extra week during fiscal 2024 versus 2025 and the ramp in hardware revenue expected during the second half of 2025.

"While we think the official guidance is close to our estimate, it is still slightly below our expectations," said Needham analysts Charles Shi and Ross Cole, in an investor note. "Coming into the print, we were expecting a 47/53 split between F1H and F2H revenues. The company guided to a 45/55 split, which makes FY25 the most F2H-weighted year in recent years for SNPS."

Needham maintains its Buy rating and $640 price target.

KeyBanc also noted that "mechanical factors" related to the fiscal 2025 outlook led to it being lower than many expected.

"Synopsys noted that without the mechanical calendar factors, FY25 revenue growth guidance would have been ~12%, a more in-line outlook," said KeyBanc analysts Jason Celino.

KeyBanc maintains its Overweight rating and a hefty $690 price target.

Synoposys competitors were varied down during early Thursday market trading as well, including Cadence Design Systems (CDNS), -3%, Arm Holdings (ARM) -0.2%, Intel (INTC) -0.1% and Siemens (OTCPK:SIEGY) +1%.

data.title: Conagra looking to sell Chef Boyardee pasta business - Reuters
data.description:
Conagra (NYSE:CAG) is looking to sell its Chef Boyardee pasta business. Conagra ticked higher by 0.6%.

Conagra (CAG), which makes Slim Jim beef jerky and Healthy Choice frozen meals, hired adviser Centerview Partners to try to sell Chef Boyardee, according to a Reuters report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

The brand may be worth $500 million in a sale and may see interest from other food makers and private equity companies, according to the report.

Conagra (CAG) is set to report Q3 results on Dec. 19.

data.title: Germany's RWE starts sale process for €2B sale of Amprion stake - Bloomberg
data.description:
German power producer RWE (OTCPK:RWEOY) has launched a sale process for its 25% stake in grid operator Amprion, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

RWE (OTCPK:RWEOY) is reaching out to potential buyers such as infrastructure and pension funds to gauge interest for the stake that is expected to be worth ~€2B ($2.1B), according to the report.

The company has been exploring options for its stake in Amprion, which operates an extra-high-voltage network in western Germany, CFO Michael Müller said in August.

Separately, RWE (OTCPK:RWEOY) reportedly is frustrated over a further planning delay to its huge U.K. Dogger Bank South offshore wind project over seabirds.

The company is said to be upset with the planning authority, which it accuses of "undermining the integrity" of proceedings through its preference to conservation groups.

data.title: Garrett Motion declares $0.06 dividend
data.description:

data.title: A Google breakup is still a possibility for the incoming EU competition boss
data.description:
The new European Union competition chief has kept the idea of a potential breakup of Google’s (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) businesses under consideration, and aims to build on the “legacy” of outgoing boss Margrethe Vestager, Bloomberg reported on Thursday.

In an interview with Bloomberg TV, incoming competition chief Teresa Ribera said divestments are one way of preventing Big Tech firms from grabbing too much market power.

“It’s something that is, of course, on the table, and we try to work together with other relevant competition authorities worldwide, including the U.S. competition authorities. It is important to take into consideration this potential division, divestment of some of these businesses. We will be assessing case by case," Ribera reportedly told Bloomberg.

Ribera also plans to build a relationship with incoming U.S. president Donald Trump, the report said. “I will try” for “a cooperative, real relationship” with the Trump administration, she said.

data.title: Ally Financial downgraded at Wolfe Research on full valuation, limited upside catalysts
data.description:
Digital financial services company Ally Financial (NYSE:ALLY) was trading lower after Wolfe Research downgraded the stock on full valuation and limited upside catalysts.

ALLY stock was 2.16% down Thursday pre-market to $38.00.

"While we continue to model ALLY's NIM reaching 4% in 2026, we expect fewer rate cuts to slow ALLY's NIM trajectory in 2025 and lower our estimates," said analyst Bill Carcache in a research note.

Higher rates may exacerbate credit stress in the near term, while pressure on originated loan yields could weigh on sentiment, said the note.

The company's tangible book value per share and common equity Tier 1 ratio is expected to reduce by ~5.5% and ~26 basis points, respectively.

"ALLY currently trades at 9.6x NTM EPS compared with its historical 1Y / 3Y / 5Y / 10Y average NTM P/E multiple of 9.5x / 7.2x / 7.7x / 8.1x and 1.0x TBV relative to its historical 1Y / 3Y / 5Y / 10Y average of 1.1x / 1.0x / 1.0x / 0.9x," said the note.

The sell-side research firm lowered its recommendation on ALLY to Peer Perform from Outperform.

Wolfe Research lowered its 2025 and 2026 EPS estimates to $3.52 and $6.23, respectively, from $3.57 and $6.48. The 2025 consensus EPS estimate is $4.39, and the 2026 consensus stands at $5.71.

The rating aligns with the Seeking Alpha authors and the Quant rating of Hold.

"Considering the swaps headwind, a positive outlook for asset quality, and an interesting valuation, I reiterate my "hold" rating on Ally's stock. The NIM outlook was solid in my previous analysis, but the valuation was too expensive. Now, it's the opposite, the valuation seems right, but the NIM recovery would be volatile," said SA analyst Daniel Urbina.

The average sell-side analysts rating on the stock is Buy.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow are muted a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq scaled to record highs
data.description:
Wall Street's major market averages were little changed on Thursday, a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq touched record highs.

Early on and the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was even, the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.21%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.17%.

Major equity averages ended at record highs on Wednesday, fronted by climbing shares for Salesforce (CRM) and other tech companies, while Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell made upbeat remarks about the world's largest economy.

"Jerome Powell signaled that the Federal Reserve would react to rather than pre-empt any consumer taxes imposed by U.S. President-elect Donald Trump via tariffs. That should allow U.S. interest rates to continue to decline in the first half of next year," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"It’s been a very eventful 24 hours for markets, with the French government losing a no-confidence vote for the first time since 1962, alongside ongoing political uncertainty in South Korea and some hawkish-leaning comments from Powell," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"But despite everything that’s happening, risk assets have been broadly unphased by the various developments," Allen added.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) also crossed the 100K milestone on Wednesday.

On the economic side, U.S. employers' announced job cuts rose 3.8% in November, climbed 27% Y/Y, while the initial jobless claims increased more than expected in past week. The two labor data came just a day ahead of the November nonfarm payrolls report.

Moreover, the Fed balance sheet is due later in the day.

data.title: Tenable CEO takes temporary medical leave of absence
data.description:
• Tenable (NASDAQ:TENB) said on Thursday that Chief Executive Officer, Amit Yoran has begun a temporary medical leave of absence from his duties to focus on his cancer treatment and recovery.
  • The board has appointed Stephen A. Vintz, the company’s CFO, and Mark Thurmond, the COO, to serve as Co-Chief Executive Officers on an interim basis.
  • Yoran will continue to serve as Chairman of the company and a member of the board during his leave.
  • Yoran announced in April that he had been diagnosed with a treatable form of cancer. He recently learned that he requires additional treatment.

data.title: Garrett Motion announces new quarterly dividend and share repurchase program
data.description:
• Garrett Motion (NASDAQ:GTX) announced the adoption of a long-term capital allocation framework, under which the company will target returning 75% or more of its adjusted free cash flow to shareholders over time through a combination of share repurchases and a regular quarterly cash dividend.
  • The company’s Board has approved a new dividend policy, pursuant to which the Board intends to declare and pay quarterly dividends on its common stock in an aggregate amount of approximately $50M in 2025.
  • The Board has declared the first quarter 2025 cash dividend on the company’s outstanding common stock of $0.06 per share payable January 31, 2025, to all common shareholders of record as of the close of business on January 15, 2025.
  • The Board has also approved a new share repurchase program for 2025, pursuant to which the company may repurchase up to $250M of its common stock throughout the course of the year.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Worksport to adopt cryptocurrency, bitcoin and XRP for corporate treasury
data.description:
• Worksport Ltd. (NASDAQ:WKSP) announced it is taking initial steps to adopt the cryptocurrencies BTC (Bitcoin) and XRP (Ripple).
  • Under the initial resolution, the board has approved the purchase of up to $5M in Bitcoin and XRP, limited to a maximum of 10% of excess operational cash.
  • The company will commit up to 10% of any excess cash from operations to purchase Bitcoin and XRP.
  • Worksport will accept cryptocurrency payments on its e-ecommerce platform. Cryptocurrency transactions are expected to cost the company up to 37% less in transaction processing fees.
  • Worksport may convert interest earnings from cash held in money market accounts into Bitcoin and XRP.
  • A designated percentage of funds raised in future capital initiatives may be allocated to long-term holdings of Bitcoin and XRP.
  • The company is also preparing to release its innovative portable power system and solar truck cover.
  • WKSP shares were up 2.4% in premarket trading on Thursday.
  • Source: Press Release
